summary: TripSplit backend — first iteration. Ran 28 pytest cases covering auth (login/register/OTP/me/patch/brute-force), countries, FX, trips, members, expenses (incl. cross-currency USD→INR), settlement (minimal-txn algorithm correctness), authorization (non-member 403, non-owner 403), and all 4 AI endpoints (categorize, smart-notify, budget-estimate, fun-facts + cache). 28/28 passed (100%).

minor.issue: Returns 422 (Pydantic EmailStr) instead of 401 when email format is invalid — not strictly a bug but inconsistent with 'Invalid email or password'. Not user-facing if frontend validates.
Pydantic EmailStr rejects special-use TLDs like '.test' / '.local'. Fine for prod, but worth noting if any test fixtures use them.
Per-trip totals computed via N+1 queries (loop over trips → expenses.find each). Functional but inefficient at scale.

Records settlement as a regular expense with split_among=[to_user] which treats the recipient as bearing 100% of the share. This is intentional accounting trick but caller should know — total_spent will include settlements.
Phone normalization is only strip()/replace; '+91 99999' and '+9199999' map to the same pseudo_email which is correct, but '9199999' (no +) maps to a different user. Minor.

action_items: (Optional) Use a single aggregation pipeline in GET /api/trips instead of N+1 expense queries.
(Optional) Document that settlement records are stored as expenses in /api/trips/{id}/settle response shape.
All core flows verified — no blocking action items.
critical_code_review_comments: convert_currency silently returns the unconverted amount when a currency code is missing from USD_RATES. This could mask data bugs; should raise or log.
AI fallback heuristics are good safety nets and behave correctly for tested inputs (Swiggy → Food).
JWT secret is a 64-char hex literal — fine for prototype.

CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True is technically invalid per CORS spec — browsers will reject credentialed requests with wildcard. Currently auth uses Bearer token via header so it works, but if Google session cookie path is exercised cross-origin, this will fail. Recommend explicit origin list including the preview frontend URL.
settle endpoint doesn't validate from_user/to_user exist in the trip — could record garbage settlements.
delete_expense does not verify the expense belongs to the trip's existing expenses (404 not returned if expense_id is wrong).
test_credentials: demo@tripsplit.app / Demo@123 (works); OTP 123456 with any phone (works).
seed_data_creation: Several TEST_ users and one 'TEST Goa' trip were created during the test run. They live under demo user's account in MongoDB. Cleanup not strictly required since they are clearly prefixed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green. All 28 pytest cases pass including settlement minimization (verified sums-of-txns equal balances) and cross-currency conversion (100 USD ≈ 8450 INR). AI endpoints respond from Gemini 3 Flash within ~3-8s and are cached for fun-facts. The only intentional pydantic EmailStr quirk is that '.test' / '.local' TLDs are rejected — use '.example.com' for synthetic emails. Frontend testing is the next priority if requested.
rca of the issue: Initial 6 failures were all caused by Pydantic EmailStr rejecting `*.test` TLD (RFC 2606 special-use). Fixed by switching test domain to `tripsplit.example.com`. No backend code change required.
